title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Thinking Of Surrendering Your ULIP? Here’s Why You Shouldn’t
Before investing in an insurance plan of any kind, it is important to ensure that it aligns with your long-term goals and objectives. This is perhaps more true in the case of Unit-Linked Insurance Plans than most others.
With the combined ULIP benefits of both investment and insurance, they are specifically suited for fulfilling the long-term goals of a variety of investors.
A ULIP investor might consider surrendering their policy well before its maturity date. To make this happen, a ULIP investor needs to be aware of how to cancel their ULIP policy.
However, it is far more important to be aware of whether surrendering a ULIP policy is an advisable move at all. Let us delve into the topic in more detail:
What Is ULIP?
Let us understand  what is ULIP and the various benefits it can offer to an investor. ULIPs are primarily designed to provide financial protection along with market linked returns. When you make an investment into a ULIP, it offers the policyholder dual benefit of investing in either  equity funds or debt funds or both while providing life insurance coverage. A professional fund manager handles the investment corpus and every ULIP investor is provided with a variety of fund options to choose from, ranging from debt to equity funds.
As an investor in a ULIP, you can choose a fund option that aligns with your long-term financial goal. For instance, the relatively safer debt funds might be ideal for those investing for their retirement. On the other hand, equity funds might be ideal for those that seek wealth creation. You also get the option to switch your investments between different ULIP funds based on your risk appetite and market conditions during the tenure of the policy.
Limitations of Surrendering a ULIP
Missed Opportunity for Long-Term Wealth Creation
ULIPs are ideal when you stay invested for a long time. Over time, even small contributions can grow significantly, adding to your wealth corpus, which is probably one of the biggest losses with early surrendering. When you surrender early (before the lock-in period of 5 years), it means that you are losing the power of compounding, especially if you choose equity fund investments.
Forfeiting Tax Advantages on Premiums and Returns
If you surrender any ULIP before its closure, you may lose out on the tax deductions that you may have claimed under Section 80C in the previous years. The maturity and final settlement benefits, which are usually tax-free under Section 10(10D), could also become taxable in the event of surrender. This can significantly reduce your final payout compared to what you would receive if the policy were continued even after the lock-in period.
Risking Your Financial Security Goals
A ULIP surrender also means that your insurance cover ends. Since wealth generation is just one aspect of any ULIP (life protection being the other one), this leaves your loved one without a safety net that covers them in case of your untimely death.
Alternatives to Surrendering Your ULIP
Before you ask yourself, what happens if I surrender my ULIP policy, consider other options to meet the need for funds. The good news is that there are several easy alternatives you can explore.
Accessing Funds Through Partial Withdrawals
Most ULIP plans allow partial withdrawals after the lock-in period of 5 years. This lets you access money for emergencies without a full ULIP policy surrender.
Optimize Returns with Fund Switching
If your current funds are not performing, you can use the fund switch option instead of surrendering. Shifting from equity to debt or vice versa can help optimize returns. This flexibility is one of the biggest advantages of ULIP plans.
How To Cancel/Surrender Your ULIP
While ULIPs may reap optimum benefits when allowed to complete its due term, an investor might consider cancelling or surrendering his ULIP policy for a variety of reasons. In such a case, it is essential to know the process of how to cancel a ULIP policy as well as the repercussions of the same.
During the 5-Year Lock-in Period
Many people ask, Can I surrender ULIP before 5 years? The answer is yes, but you won’t get the payout immediately. Your money moves to a discontinued policy fund, where it earns a small interest until the lock-in ends. Discontinuance charges and lost growth mean you’ll receive far less than expected.
After 5-Year Lock-in Period
A ULIP surrender after 5 years allows you to withdraw the full fund value without discontinuance charges. Exiting too soon can still mean giving up the growth potential and tax advantages that come with staying invested.
Reasons To Hold onto Your ULIP Investment
Continuing your ULIP investment can be far more rewarding than surrendering.
Tax benefits: Premiums are deductible under Section 80C (old regime), and maturity/death benefits are tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to conditions.
Wealth creation: Market-linked returns grow substantially over time.
Insurance protection: You maintain life cover for your family.
Flexibility: Fund switches and partial withdrawals can handle short-term needs.
Avoid penalties: No ULIP surrender charges to affect your savings.
Conclusion
ULIPs serve their purpose when they are utilised for a long-term goal. This allows them to build market-linked returns on investment and allows the investor to enjoy the accompanying tax benefits and coverage for a longer duration of time. Therefore, if a withdrawal or surrendering from a ULIP is not urgent or necessary, it is recommended that the ULIP be allowed to benefit from investment compounding and yield its returns at the end of the policy tenure.
FAQs
How does surrendering a ULIP affect long-term wealth creation?
Surrendering a ULIP disrupts your long-term financial plan. These policies are designed to grow your wealth using the power of compounding. If you surrender the plan, you lose this benefit entirely and may even receive a much smaller payout after ULIP surrender charges are deducted.
Do I lose tax benefits if I surrender my ULIP?
Deductions under Section 80C may no longer remain valid, and the payout you receive could be taxable. The taxability of ULIP on surrender can leave you with far less money than you initially expected.
Can surrendering a ULIP impact my financial security goals?
ULIP plans provide both life cover and wealth creation. If you surrender, you lose the insurance protection for your family and reduce the funds available for long-term goals like home ownership or children’s education. A ULIP surrender often sets your financial planning back by years.
How can I access funds without surrendering my ULIP?
Instead of surrendering, you can make partial or complete withdrawals after the five-year lock-in period. This option allows you to meet urgent needs without cancelling your policy. You could also switch between equity and debt funds to optimize returns rather than choosing a complete ULIP surrender.
Why is it beneficial to stay invested in a ULIP?
ULIP plans are structured for long-term wealth growth. Staying invested helps you benefit from compounding and market-linked returns while keeping your life cover active. You also continue to enjoy tax deductions and exemptions. Exiting early through a ULIP surrender forfeits these advantages and reduces your financial security.
Disclaimer:
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy
full_pdf_text:
Thinking Of Surrendering Your ULIP? Here’s Why You Shouldn’t
Before investing in an insurance plan of any kind, it is important to ensure that it aligns with your long- term goals and objectives. This is perhaps more true in the case of Unit-Linked Insurance Plans than most others.
With the combined ULIP benefits of both investment and insurance, they are specifically suited for fulfilling the long-term goals of a variety of investors.
A ULIP investor might consider surrendering their policy well before its maturity date. To make this
happen, a ULIP investor needs to be aware of how to cancel their ULIP policy.
However, it is far more important to be aware of whether surrendering a ULIP policy is an advisable
move at all. Let us delve into the topic in more detail:
What Is ULIP?
Let us understand what is ULIP and the various benefits it can offer to an investor. ULIPs are primarily
designed to provide financial protection along with market linked returns. When you make an
investment into a ULIP, it offers the policyholder dual benefit of investing in either equity funds or
debt funds or both while providing life insurance coverage. A professional fund manager handles the
investment corpus and every ULIP investor is provided with a variety of fund options to choose from,
ranging from debt to equity funds.
As an investor in a ULIP, you can choose a fund option that aligns with your long-term financial goal. For
instance, the relatively safer debt funds might be ideal for those investing for their retirement. On the
other hand, equity funds might be ideal for those that seek wealth creation. You also get the option to
switch your investments between different ULIP funds based on your risk appetite and market
conditions during the tenure of the policy.
Limitations of Surrendering a ULIP
Missed Opportunity for Long-Term Wealth Creation
ULIPs are suitable when you stay invested for a long time. Over time, small contributions have the potential to grow through the power of compounding , depending on market performance. . When you surrender early (before the lock-in period of 5 years), it means that you are not allowing your savings to grow with compounding, which takes time.
Forfeiting Tax Advantages on Premiums and Returns
If you surrender any ULIP before its closure, you may lose out on the tax deductions that you may have claimed under Section 80C in the previous years. The policy benefits, which are usually tax-free under Section 10(10D), could also become taxable in the event of surrender. This can significantly reduce your final payout compared to what you would receive if the policy were continued .
Risking Your Financial Security Goals
A ULIP surrender also means that your life cover ends , this leaves your loved one without a financial safety net that covers them in case of your untimely death.
Alternatives to Surrendering Your ULIP
Before you ask yourself, what happens if I surrender my ULIP policy, consider other options to meet the need for money . The good news is that there are several easy alternatives you can explore like partial withdrawals.
Accessing Funds Through Partial Withdrawals
ULIP plans allow partial withdrawals after the lock-in period of 5 years. This lets you access money for emergencies without a full ULIP policy surrender.
Optimize Returns with Fund Switching
If your current market linked funds are delivering lower returns , you can use the fund switch option instead of surrendering. Shifting from equity to debt or vice versa can help optimize returns depending on market performance. This flexibility is one of the biggest advantages of ULIP plans.
What Happens When You Cancel/Surrender Your ULIP
While ULIPs may reap optimum benefits when allowed to complete its due term, an investor might
consider cancelling or surrendering his ULIP policy for a variety of reasons. In such a case, it is essential
to know the process of how to cancel a ULIP policy as well as the repercussions of the same.
During the 5-Year Lock-in Period
Many people ask, Can I surrender ULIP before 5 years? The answer is yes, but you won’t get the payout immediately. Your money moves to a discontinued policy fund, where it earns a minimum interest rate until the lock-in ends. Discontinuance charges and lower returns mean you’ll receive far less than expected.
After 5-Year Lock-in Period
A ULIP surrender after 5 years allows you to withdraw the fund value without discontinuance charges. Exiting too soon can still mean giving up the growth potential and tax advantages that come with staying invested.
Reasons To Hold onto Your ULIP Investment
Continuing your ULIP investment can be far more rewarding than surrendering.
● Tax benefits: Premiums are deductible under Section 80C (old regime), and maturity/death benefits are tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to conditions. ● Potential of Wealth creation: Wealth creation through Market-linked returns (subject to performance of the underlying fund) . ● Life Insurance protection: You maintain life cover for your family. ● Flexibility: Fund switches and partial withdrawals can handle short-term needs. ●
Conclusion
ULIPs serve their purpose when they are utilised for a long-term goal. This allows them to build market-
linked returns on investment and allows the investor to enjoy the accompanying tax benefits and
coverage for a longer duration of time. Therefore, if a withdrawal or surrendering from a ULIP is not
urgent or necessary, it is recommended that the ULIP be allowed to benefit from investment
compounding and yield its returns at the end of the policy tenure.
FAQs
How does surrendering a ULIP affect long-term wealth creation? Surrendering a ULIP disrupts your long-term financial plan. These plans give the potential of wealth creation using the power of compounding. If you surrender the plan, you lose this benefit entirely and may even receive a much smaller payout after ULIP surrender charges are deducted. Do I lose tax benefits if I surrender my ULIP? Deductions under Section 80C may no longer remain valid, and the payout you receive could be taxable. . Can surrendering a ULIP impact my financial security goals? ULIP plans provide both life cover and the potential of wealth creation through market linked funds. If you surrender, you lose the financial protection for your family and limit the corpus
available for long-term goals like home ownership or children’s education. A ULIP surrender often sets your financial planning back by years. How can I access part of my investments without surrendering my ULIP? Instead of surrendering, you can make partial after the five-year lock-in period. This option allows you to meet urgent needs without cancelling your policy. Why is it beneficial to stay invested in a ULIP? ULIP plans are life insurance plans that are structured for long-term wealth growth. Staying invested helps you stay protected longer and benefit from compounding and market-linked returns. You also continue to enjoy tax deductions and exemptions. Exiting early through a ULIP surrender forfeits these advantages and reduces your financial security. Disclaimer:
